strengths: 10+ years fullstack experience with recent TypeScript/Node.js/React product work (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq)
Direct AI agent development experience — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at current role
Strong testing/craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, authored test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught testing courses
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
No Kotlin and only limited/dated Java exposure (robotic arms at Altom, 2016-2018)
Vitest/Jest/Playwright not named explicitly, despite heavy general testing emphasis

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and Kotlin from primary/secondary stack

response.justification: The candidate exceeds the 6+ years requirement with roughly 15 years of experience, including recent, directly relevant TypeScript/Node.js/React work at Optilyz (pricing platform with MongoDB and AWS CDK) and RIDE (Node/TypeScript/GraphQL with TDD and trunk-based development). The AI agent requirement — often the hardest to fill — is concretely evidenced by MCP code generator and agentic-framework work at re:cinq. Database (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes via GISCollective), Git, and cloud (AWS) requirements are all evidenced. Testing expertise is exceptional in spirit (TDD advocacy, authored two test frameworks, Selenium course) though the specific tools Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not named. Gaps are narrow: Nest.js is absent, Kotlin is absent, and Java evidence is dated — but these are secondary-stack items and the breadth of languages demonstrated (TypeScript, D, C++, Java, Swift, PHP) suggests fast ramp-up. A CS bachelor's satisfies the education requirement.
